product.name: ArWoWear T7 Chem Pro
product.categorySourceGroup: Vat Dyed Workwear Fabrics Resistant to Industrial Chemicals
product.titleDescriptor: 60% Cotton 40% Polyester, Chemical Resistant Fabric, 245 gr/m'2
product.specLine: 245 g/m², 60% Cotton 40% Polyester, VAT Dyed, Chemical Resistant, 3/1S Twill Fabric
descriptions: ArWoWear T7 Chem Pro is 245 g/m², vat dyed, 60% cotton 40% polyester merserized workwear fabric with ultra comfort.
Water and oil repellent (TS EN ISO 4920) function.
It is resistant to most industrial chemicals. (EN ISO 13034)
60% Cotton 40% Polyester, Chemical Resistant Fabric, 245 gr/m'2
245 g/m², 60% Cotton 40% Polyester, VAT Dyed, Chemical Resistant, 3/1S Twill Fabric
technicalParameters: Tensile Strength | Warp>1000N; Weft>800N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>20N; Weft>15N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Product has 'emi' property (EMI shielding) which could relate to electrical/electronic environments, but no explicit statement of energy sector or high-voltage/electrical utility use is given.
Chemical resistance and workwear coating could plausibly relate to industrial metalworking hazards, but no explicit welding, foundry or metal-splash use is stated.

stage1.noMatchReason: This is a general chemical-resistant workwear fabric (coveralls application) tested for chemical resistance (EN 13034) and OEKO-TEX certification, with an EMI property listed. There is no explicit statement tying it to military, firefighting, energy, welding, motorcycle, hivis, medical, sport, architecture-building or transport use cases.

evidence: categorySourceGroup: Vat Dyed Workwear Fabrics Resistant to Industrial Chemicals
It is resistant to most industrial chemicals.
primary.rationale: The fabric is explicitly described as chemical-resistant workwear for coveralls, a general industrial protective apparel use with no narrower sector (e.g., welding, energy) explicitly stated despite the EMI property.

secondary.rationale: No independently stated secondary application; EMI property alone does not justify energy sector per rule 8, and chemical resistance does not justify welding per rule 9.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: EMI property alone is insufficient without explicit electrical/utility use statement.
Chemical resistance does not equate to welding/metal-splash hazard; no explicit welding or foundry use stated.

It is resistant to most industrial chemicals.
